title: USD Layer Stack Open Contract

included: open one resolved root layer resource
read subLayers and paired subLayerOffsets from the root and recursively opened local sublayers
local authoring-layer-relative resolution for sublayer asset identifiers
open recursively discovered local sublayer resources through usd-layer-open
detect recursive sublayer cycles by resolved layer location
construct a LayerStackNamespaceSource from opened root and recursive sublayers
surface root-open, sublayer-open, unsupported-resource, sublayer-cycle, and layer-stack diagnostics
adapter validation with temporary local USDA resources
deferred: full asset resolver search paths, callbacks, contexts, identifiers, and repository policy
network or remote protocol resource loading
references, payloads, inherits, specializes, variants, relocates, and value clips
attribute value resolution and subLayerOffsets retiming

model.LayerStackOpenResult: Target-native result containing either a LayerStackNamespaceSource or diagnostics. It is not canonical dump JSON.
model.RootLayerResource: Resolved local root layer resource supplied by a caller or adapter.
model.SublayerResource: An authored subLayer asset identifier resolved by this contract's local policy and opened through usd-layer-open.
model.LocalResolutionPolicy: Prototype policy that resolves plain and ./ or ../ relative local sublayer identifiers against the containing directory of the layer that authored the subLayer.
model.OpenedLayerSet: The root document-model Layer and recursively discovered local sublayer document-model Layers returned by usd-layer-open.
model.ComposedSource: The LayerStackNamespaceSource constructed from the opened layer set.

input.rootLocation: resolved local root layer resource location

output.result: target-native LayerStackOpenResult containing a LayerStackNamespaceSource or diagnostics

input.authoringLayerLocation: resolved local resource location of the layer that authored the subLayer

output.resolvedLocation: resolved local sublayer location or diagnostic

output.layers: strong-to-weak opened layer entries with identifier, resolvedLocation, strengthIndex, offset, and scale; recursive sublayers appear depth-first immediately after the layer that authored them

output.sourceResult: same shape as layer-stack namespace-source prim_input
same shape as layer-stack namespace-source property_input

laws: root and sublayer resources must be opened through usd-layer-open
the loader must not parse USDA text directly or maintain parser-local syntax trees
successful layer-open results must be passed as document-model Layers to usd-layer-stack-namespace-source
the loader must not duplicate layer-stack composition semantics already owned by usd-layer-stack-namespace-source
plain relative and anchored relative local sublayer identifiers are resolved against the containing directory of the layer that authored the subLayer for this prototype contract
unsupported URI schemes must fail clearly and must not fall back to local filesystem strings
missing sublayer resources must fail clearly and include the authored asset identifier
recursive sublayer cycles must fail clearly, include the authored asset identifier that would close the cycle, and must not recurse unboundedly
recursive sublayers are ordered depth-first at the authored subLayer site, before later sibling sublayers from the same authoring layer
subLayerOffsets are passed through to the layer-stack namespace source but are not applied to values

adapter JSON may create temporary local resources for conformance tests, but domain APIs exchange resolved locations, LayerOpenResult values, document-model Layers, and LayerStackNamespaceSource objects

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with rootLocation, optional resources mapping temporary relative resource paths to USDA text, and an ops array. The adapter may materialize resources in a temporary directory before calling the domain loader.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
